content.method: Administrative data analysis of current admissions composition by SES and geography; natural or field-experimental variation in admissions-test design (e.g., piloting reduced-tutoring-dependent assessment formats in a subset of schools) with pre/post composition comparison.
content.question: What proportion of students in selective gimnaziums come from disadvantaged versus middle-class/affluent backgrounds, and how would this composition shift under admissions-criteria reform (T3) that reduces tutoring-sensitivity, without abolishing the tracks?
content.why_it_matters: This is the key empirical question for assessing whether T3 can meaningfully improve equity within the existing structure, as an alternative or complement to structural abolition, and whether it risks merely reshuffling which affluent families gain access rather than expanding disadvantaged access.
